mcqs.question: In which multithreading model are multiple user threads mapped to a single kernel thread?
mcqs.explanation: The Many-to-One model maps multiple user threads to a single kernel thread. All thread management is done in user space by the thread library.

mcqs.question: What happens if a user thread makes a blocking system call in the Many-to-One model?

options.text: All user threads mapped to that kernel thread block
The kernel selects another user thread

mcqs.explanation: In Many-to-One model, all user threads are mapped to a single kernel thread, so if any thread makes a blocking system call, all threads block.

mcqs.question: Which model allows true parallel execution on multiprocessor systems?
mcqs.explanation: One-to-One model allows true parallel execution because each user thread has its own kernel thread that can run on different processors.

mcqs.question: The Many-to-Many model multiplexes:

options.text: Multiple user threads to multiple kernel threads
Multiple kernel threads to a single user thread
Single user thread to multiple kernel threads
Single kernel thread to multiple user threads

mcqs.explanation: Many-to-Many multiplexes multiple user threads to an equal or lesser number of kernel threads, allowing efficient resource utilization.

mcqs.question: Which is NOT a thread library?
mcqs.explanation: TCP/IP Sockets is a networking API, not a thread library. POSIX Pthreads, Win32 API, and Java Threads are all thread libraries.

mcqs.question: In the Two-level Model, some user threads can be bound to dedicated kernel threads. This is similar to:
mcqs.explanation: When user threads are bound to dedicated kernel threads in Two-level Model, it functions like One-to-One Model.

mcqs.question: What is the main disadvantage of the One-to-One model?
mcqs.explanation: The main disadvantage of One-to-One is overhead of creating and managing many kernel threads, which can limit the number of concurrent threads.

mcqs.question: Round Robin scheduling uses:
mcqs.explanation: Round Robin uses time slicing where each thread gets a fixed time quantum to execute in a cyclic order.

mcqs.question: Which scheduling algorithm minimizes average waiting time?
mcqs.explanation: SJF (Shortest Job First) provides optimal average waiting time when the burst times are known in advance.

mcqs.question: What problem does the convoy effect occur in?
mcqs.explanation: Convoy effect occurs in FCFS where short processes wait behind long processes, leading to poor CPU utilization.

mcqs.question: What is thread affinity in multiprocessor scheduling?

options.text: Keep a thread running on the same processor for cache efficiency
Bind thread to highest priority processor
Distribute threads equally to all processors

mcqs.explanation: Thread affinity tries to keep a thread on the same processor to improve cache hit rate and performance.

mcqs.question: Which scheduling criterion focuses on minimizing the time from request to first response?
mcqs.explanation: Response Time is the time from request submission to when the first response is produced, important for interactive systems.

mcqs.question: What is aging in the context of scheduling?

options.text: Increasing priority of processes waiting in queue over time

mcqs.explanation: Aging is a technique where the priority of waiting processes is gradually increased to prevent starvation of low-priority processes.

mcqs.question: In which model does the thread library handle all thread management without kernel awareness?
mcqs.explanation: User-level threads are entirely managed by the thread library in user space, with no kernel involvement in thread scheduling.

mcqs.question: What is the primary benefit of the Many-to-Many model over One-to-One?

options.text: Better parallel performance on single processor
Lower overhead than creating many kernel threads

mcqs.explanation: Many-to-Many has lower overhead than One-to-One because it uses fewer kernel threads than user threads.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a preemptive scheduling algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: Round Robin is preemptive as it forcibly removes a thread after its time quantum expires. FCFS and non-preemptive SJF are non-preemptive.

mcqs.question: What happens to thread scheduling when multiple processors are available?

options.text: Only one processor can be used

mcqs.explanation: In multiprocessor systems, load balancing ensures work is distributed evenly across processors for optimal performance.

mcqs.question: A thread library that follows POSIX standards is called:
mcqs.explanation: POSIX Threads (Pthreads) is the POSIX standard API for thread creation and management.

mcqs.question: What is the time quantum in Round Robin scheduling?

options.text: Fixed time slice allocated to each thread
Based on burst time of thread

mcqs.explanation: Round Robin uses a fixed time quantum that is allocated to each thread in the ready queue.

mcqs.question: Which multithreading model is implemented in Linux and Windows?
mcqs.explanation: Modern operating systems like Linux and Windows typically use the One-to-One model, where each user thread maps to a kernel thread.
